import re,pathlib
files=list(pathlib.Path('src').rglob('*.java'))
def strip_comments(s):
    s=re.sub(r'/\\*.*?\\*/', lambda m:'\\n'*m.group(0).count('\\n'), s, flags=re.S)
    s=re.sub(r'//.*','',s)
    return s
# simple method finder with brace extents
method_pat=re.compile(r'((?:public|protected|private|static|final|abstract|synchronized|native|\\s)+)(?:<["'^>]+>'"\\s*)?([\\w\\[\\]<>?, .]+?)\\s+(\\w+)\\s*\\((.*?)\\)\\s*(?:throws["'^'"\\{;]*)?\\{', re.S)
def find_methods(s):
    methods=[]
    for m in method_pat.finditer(s):
        # skip control keywords by name
        if m.group(3) in ('if','for','while','switch','catch','return','new'): continue
        b=m.end()-1; depth=0; end=len(s)
        for i in range(b,len(s)):
            if s[i]=='{': depth+=1
            elif s[i]=='}':
                depth-=1
                if depth==0: end=i+1; break
        methods.append((m.start(),end,m.group(3)))
    return methods
for p in files:
    s=strip_comments(p.read_text(errors='ignore'))
    if '.serialize' not in s: continue
    methods=find_methods(s)
    for m in re.finditer(r'\\.serialize\\s*\\(', s):
        # count args until matching )
        start=s.find('(',m.start())+1; depth=1; i=start
        while i<len(s) and depth:
            if s[i]=='(': depth+=1
            elif s[i]==')': depth-=1
            i+=1
        args=s[start:i-1]
        # split top-level
        d=0; n=1 if args.strip() else 0
        for ch in args:
            if ch==',' and d==0: n+=1
            elif ch in '(<[': d+=1
            elif ch in ')>]' and d>0: d-=1
        if n==3:
            meth=[mm for mm in methods if mm[0] < m.start() < mm[1]]
            name=meth[-1][2] if meth else '<init/block>'
            line=s.count('\\n',0,m.start())+1
            print(f'{p}:{line}:{name}: {s[m.start()-60:m.start()+80].strip().replace(chr(10),\" \")}')
